complete_employment_timeline.title: State Trooper / K-9 Handler
complete_employment_timeline.notes: Active sworn law enforcement officer. Partnered with K-9 Duke for narcotics detection.

previous_candidacies.outcome: Won 3-way race with 44.6% (475 votes), unseating incumbent Billy Ridgeway

criminal_and_law_enforcement_record.open_source_review_conclusion: No records found in open-source review. Clean criminal record as of research date.

conflicts_of_interest_inventory.description: Active sworn law enforcement officer (Texas DPS Trooper) serving simultaneously as Board President of a public school district. While not a legal conflict under Texas law, the dual role of armed state authority and school board leadership creates a power-dynamic concern, particularly regarding campus safety policy, SRO contracts, and discipline decisions where law enforcement perspective may dominate.

about_public_record.about_summary_narrative:
Jacob Muehlstein is the Board President of Harleton ISD, serving Place 7 since November 2018. He is an active Texas Department of Public Safety Trooper and K-9 handler, partnered with K-9 Duke for narcotics detection.

Muehlstein won his seat in a competitive three-way race in 2018 with 44.6% of the vote (475 votes), unseating incumbent Billy Ridgeway. That same election saw a $10.5 million bond proposition fail.

His dual role as an active sworn law enforcement officer and school board president is noted as a low-severity concern: while not a legal conflict, it creates a unique power dynamic in a small rural district. No negative public-record items have surfaced as of this research date.

education_policy_positions.bond_and_budget: On board when $10.5M bond failed Nov 2018 (was elected same cycle; position on bond REQUIRES_FURTHER_EVIDENCE)

red_flags.description: Active sworn law enforcement officer (Texas DPS Trooper, K-9 handler) serving simultaneously as Board President. Dual role creates a power-dynamic concern in a small rural district, particularly regarding campus safety policy and discipline decisions.

summary: Jacob Muehlstein is the Board President of Harleton ISD (Place 7). Active Texas DPS Trooper and K-9 handler (partnered with K-9 Duke for narcotics detection). Won a 3-way race in Nov 2018 with 44.6% (475 votes), unseating incumbent Billy Ridgeway. On the November 2026 ballot. Clean criminal record in open-source review.
analyst_notes: Law enforcement background is the defining feature of this trustee. In a small district (~705 students), the board president being an active DPS trooper carries informal authority beyond the board seat. Track whether safety and discipline policy reflects professional bias. The 2018 race was competitive (3-way, plurality win), suggesting he was not the establishment choice at the time. Now entrenched as board president after 8 years. Priority next moves: locate social media, pull Harrison CAD property records, pull voter roll primary history, check for any internal affairs or TCOLE complaints (public records in TX), pull meeting minutes for attendance and vote patterns.
research_gaps: Harrison County voter roll primary history
TCOLE (Texas Commission on Law Enforcement) license verification and complaint check
DPS internal affairs records (if any, via TPIA request)
Harleton ISD board meeting minutes for attendance and vote patterns
Campaign finance filings for 2018 and 2026 cycles
Identify opponents in 2018 race beyond Billy Ridgeway (third candidate name)
Bond failure details and Muehlstein's stated position on the $10.5M bond
